extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts and ends near a medium level (around 0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is a long, single low plateau in the middle (values drop to around 0.2–0.25 between roughly indices 50–100).  
R3: Near the end of the series, there is one short, sharp positive spike that rises clearly above the medium level (around or above 0.75) before returning to the medium level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a medium–high level (around 0.47–0.5).  
R2: There is one long high plateau in the middle (values rise quickly to around 0.8–0.82 and stay there between roughly indices 50–100).  
R3: There are no deep dips or sharp spikes outside this single central high plateau; the baseline before and after it remains roughly flat.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series starts and ends near a medium level (around 0.47–0.5).  
R2: There is a wide central low plateau (values drop to around 0.16–0.2 between roughly indices 40–110).  
R3: Inside or at the edge of this low region, there is at least one very sharp additional dip (a narrow valley falling even lower than the plateau) before the signal returns to the starting medium level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts and ends around a mid-level plateau (~0.45–0.5) with small noise.  
R2: There is one long, central low segment (~0.2–0.25) roughly between indices 50–100.  
R3: Inside the second half (after index ~100) there is a single pronounced spike that rises clearly above the surrounding plateau (~0.75–0.8) and then quickly returns.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends around a mid–high level (~0.47–0.5) with small noise.  
R2: There is one broad, central high plateau (~0.8–0.82) between about indices 50–100.  
R3: Outside this central plateau (before ~50 and after ~100) the signal remains relatively flat and never drops very low (stays above ~0.45).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series starts and ends around a mid-level plateau (~0.47–0.5) with small noise.  
R2: There is one long, central low segment (~0.16–0.2) roughly between indices 40–100.  
R3: Within or near the right side of this low segment there is a very sharp, deep dip (narrow valley) that briefly goes even lower than the plateau minimum before the signal rises back up.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts near 0.5, then drops around index 50 to a low plateau near 0.2–0.25.  
R2: Rises back toward 0.5 around index 100.  
R3: Contains a single short positive spike above 0.7 after it has risen from the low plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts near 0.48–0.5.  
R2: Quickly rises around index 50 to a high plateau near 0.8–0.82 and stays roughly flat.  
R3: Drops back to around 0.48–0.5 around index 100 and remains there without extra spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: Starts near 0.48–0.5, then drops around index 40–50 to a low plateau near 0.15–0.2.  
R2: Rises back near 0.48–0.5 around index 100.  
R3: Contains a short deep dip down to about 0.15–0.2 after this rise, then returns again near 0.48–0.5.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts and ends at a mid‑high level around 0.45–0.55.  
R2: There is one wide, flat low plateau (≈0.20–0.25) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: After returning from the low plateau to the mid‑high level, there is a single short positive spike clearly higher than the rest (around 0.75–0.80).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a mid level around 0.45–0.50.  
R2: There is one wide, flat high plateau (≈0.80–0.82) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: The transition up to the high plateau and down from it are both single, steep edges with no additional spikes or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series starts and ends at a mid level around 0.45–0.50.  
R2: There is one wide, flat low plateau (≈0.15–0.20) after the beginning and before the end.  
R3: After leaving this low plateau, there is a very deep, narrow dip (below the plateau’s level) shortly before the final return to the mid level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Signal starts and ends around a mid-high level (~0.45–0.55).  
R2: Contains one long, flat low plateau (~0.20–0.25) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: Contains one short, sharp positive spike above the baseline (>0.70) after the low plateau, then returns to the mid-high level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Signal starts and ends around a mid-level (~0.45–0.50).  
R2: Contains one long, flat high plateau (~0.80–0.83) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: No pronounced spikes above the plateau or dips below the baseline outside the main high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: Signal starts and ends around a mid-high level (~0.45–0.50).  
R2: Contains one long, flat low plateau (~0.16–0.20) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: During or just after the low plateau, contains an additional sharp dip to an even lower value (~0.15) before returning to the mid-high level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal starts and ends around a medium level (about 0.45–0.55).  
R2: There is a single long, wide dip to a clearly lower level (about 0.2–0.3) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: Near the end, there is one short spike above the starting level (up to about 0.7–0.8) that returns quickly to the medium level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal starts and ends around a medium level (about 0.45–0.55).  
R2: There is one long, wide plateau at a distinctly higher level (about 0.8) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: Apart from this plateau, the signal remains close to the medium level without strong dips or spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal starts and ends around a medium level (about 0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is one long, wide dip to a low level (about 0.15–0.25) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: Inside or near the end of this low region, there is an additional very sharp, deep dip (even lower than the main dip) before the signal returns to the medium level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal starts and ends around a mid‑level value (roughly 0.45–0.55) with only small noise.
R2: In the middle part, the signal drops to a clearly lower plateau (roughly 0.20–0.30) and stays low for a sustained interval.
R3: After the low plateau, there is a brief, sharp peak to a clearly higher value (around 0.75–0.80) before returning to the mid level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal starts and ends around a mid‑to‑low value (roughly 0.45–0.50) with small noise.
R2: In the middle part, the signal rises quickly to a higher plateau (roughly 0.80–0.83) and remains high and flat for a long continuous interval.
R3: There are no pronounced extra spikes or dips beyond the single main high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal starts and ends around a mid value (roughly 0.45–0.50) with small noise.
R2: In the first middle segment, the signal drops to a low plateau (roughly 0.15–0.20) and stays low for a sustained interval.
R3: After returning near the initial mid level, the signal exhibits an additional very deep and narrow dip (lower than the first plateau) before finally returning to the mid level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts and ends at a medium level (around 0.45–0.5).
R2: There is one long, wide dip to a low level (~0.2–0.25) in the middle of the series.
R3: After the dip, there is a single narrow peak clearly higher than the start/end level (around 0.75–0.8) before returning to the medium level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a medium level (around 0.47–0.5).
R2: There is one long, wide plateau at a clearly higher, almost constant level (~0.8) in the middle of the series.
R3: Apart from the single high plateau, the curve stays roughly flat at the medium level with small noise.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series starts and ends at a medium-high level (around 0.47–0.5).
R2: There is one long, wide dip to a very low level (~0.15–0.2) in the middle of the series.
R3: Inside or just after this low region, there is at least one very narrow, deep valley or spike that briefly goes even lower than the surrounding low segment before returning to the medium-high level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts and ends near a mid-level value (around 0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is a single long low “valley” in the middle (values drop to about 0.2–0.25 for a sustained period).  
R3: In the last third, after returning to mid-level, there is one short, sharp “peak” above the mid-level (around 0.75–0.8).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a lower baseline (around 0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is one long, flat “plateau” at a clearly higher level (around 0.8) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: There are no pronounced spikes or dips inside this plateau; it stays roughly constant until it drops back to the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series starts and ends near a mid-level value (around 0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is one long, central low “valley” (values around 0.15–0.2) similar in position and duration to Class 0/1 transitions.  
R3: Within or at the edge of this valley region there is an additional very sharp, deep dip that briefly goes even lower than the valley, before the signal returns to the mid-level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Signal starts and ends around a mid‑level (~0.45–0.5).
R2: Contains one long low plateau in the middle (~0.2–0.25) starting near index 50 and ending near index 100.
R3: After the low plateau, there is a single short, clear peak (significantly above start level, e.g. >0.7) before returning to the mid‑level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Signal starts and ends around a mid‑high level (~0.47–0.5).
R2: Contains one long high plateau (~0.8) in the middle, lasting from about index 40 to 100.
R3: No pronounced peaks higher than this plateau; the top is flat/rectangular, then drops back to the start level.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: Signal starts and ends around a mid‑level (~0.47–0.5).
R2: Contains one long low plateau (~0.17–0.2) in the middle, similar in duration and position to Class 0’s low plateau.
R3: Within or just after this low region, there is an additional deep, narrow dip (even lower than the main low, around ~0.15) before the signal returns to the starting level.
